output.final_deliverable:
I could not complete a 4+ Wikipedia-URL chain because only the initial visit succeeded. What I can verify from the merged results:

- Start (visited): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (visit succeeded). Source: merged result "Visit Wikipedia Main Page (start)".
- Links found on that page (links_sample from the visit result):
  - https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Main_Page
  - https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Main_Page
  - https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Main_Page
  (These three URLs are listed in the visit result's links_sample.)

What failed / is missing (so I cannot build the required chain):
- The subsequent visit steps (Page2, Page3, Page4) all failed because the Visit nodes were missing valid URL fields. The merged results show errors like: "Visit node missing valid URL. Node title: 'Visit Page2 — follow a link found on Main Page'. Details should contain 'url' or 'link' field with a valid ..." (for Page2, Page3, Page4). Because those nodes did not supply the exact URLs chosen from the previous page, I was unable to fetch the next pages and therefore could not extract links from them.

Consequences:
- I cannot produce a chain of 4+ Wikipedia URLs where each next URL is taken from links on the previous page because the evidence contains only the Main Page visit and a small sample of links from it. The required exact URLs for Page2, Page3, and Page4 (and the visited pages' link lists) are missing.
- I cannot provide a verified adjacency pair (URL[i] -> URL[i+1]) near the end because the necessary follow-up page visits and link extractions were not available in the merged results.

If you want me to complete the chain, please provide or allow the visit results for Page2 (an exact URL chosen from Main Page), Page3 (exact URL chosen from Page2), and Page4 (exact URL chosen from Page3), or re-run the visit actions so I can follow the links and verify adjacency pairs.

Citations (from merged results):
- Start page visited: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (merged result: "Visit Wikipedia Main Page (start)").
- Sample links extracted on that page: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Main_Page , https://en.wikivoyage.org/wiki/Main_Page , https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Main_Page (from the same merged visit result).
output.action_summary: Only the initial visit to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page succeeded (links_sample contains three URLs). Subsequent visit steps for Page2, Page3, and Page4 failed because their Visit nodes were missing valid URL fields, so I could not follow links to build a 4+ URL chain or verify any adjacency pair. To complete the task I need the exact URLs (Page2, Page3, Page4) or successful visits for those pages.

result.title: Verify adjacency pair (show Page3 links to Page4) and assemble final response

details.intent: Using extracted link lists from the Page3 visit, verify and present one adjacency pair near the end of the chain by finding the exact Page4 URL inside Page3's extracted _links_inline evidence.
target_facts: A concrete evidence snippet showing that Page3's extracted links include the exact Page4 URL (the '_links_inline' string that contains '[link: https://...Page4...]').
Final ordered chain array of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs with link text/title and exact URLs.
verification_steps: Look at the Page3 visit output's _links_inline field (array of strings with embedded [link: ...] tokens).
Find an entry where the embedded URL exactly matches Page4_url selected earlier.
Extract the exact snippet (the _links_inline entry) that demonstrates the link (this is the verifiable adjacency pair: Page3 -> Page4).
If the chosen Page4_url is not present in Page3's links, fall back to another Page4 candidate from Page3's link list and repeat until an adjacency is proven; only then finalize the chain.
details.extraction_plan: Analyze Page3's extracted links; return a short evidence block containing the Page3 link text and the exact [link: URL] token proving the adjacency. Then output the final chain ordered list: [Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page), Page2 (title + url), Page3 (title + url), Page4 (title + url)].
details.justification: A think/verification step is needed to transform the raw visited-page outputs into the final answer and to provide the explicit, verifiable adjacency pair demanded by the user. This step depends on earlier visits' outputs, so it is sequential and ensures the chain is provably linked.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

details.intent: Read the Main Page and extract inline internal Wikipedia links (link text + exact URL) to choose the next page in the chain.
target_facts: A list of internal Wikipedia links found on the Main Page (each: link_text, exact_url).
Select a candidate URL (Page2_url) from that list to visit next; record its link_text and exact_url.
details.extraction_plan: Download full page content. Extract all _links_inline entries (they will be formatted like 'Link text [link: https://en.wikipedia.org/... ]'). Capture the first 10 distinct internal wiki links from the main content area (ignore external sites, non-article special links). Output: array of {link_text, url}. Mark the chosen link as Page2_url and include rationale for choice (e.g., first suitable article link).
details.justification: This visit is required by the mandate (start at Main Page). It produces the exact URLs (in _links_inline) that subsequent visit nodes will consume, enforcing the rule that visit nodes use URLs produced by previous nodes. This reduces uncertainty by providing verifiable source URLs.
